ogTitle: Blyth vs Boatswain Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Blyth and Boatswain Point.
heading: Blyth vs Boatswain Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Blyth, South Australia and Boatswain Point, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Blyth has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Boatswain Point (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Blyth or Boatswain Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Blyth has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Boatswain Point scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Blyth scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Boatswain Point scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Blyth or Boatswain Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Blyth has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
